dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal emphasizes discipline in waiting for clear trend conditions (price > MA + ADX confirmation). Entry requires price break of 10-period MA with ADX > 15 indicating trend strength. Exit triggers on ADX collapse below 10, which signals trend exhaustion. The 5-consecutive-bar ADX condition cannot be precisely encoded in this 3-field rule structure, so exit uses single ADX < 10 condition as practical approximation. Direction is context-dependent as signal applies to both long and short trends based on trend direction. Swing low/high structural break exit cannot be expressed without price history arrays, so ADX-based exit serves as primary exit signal.

source_rule_text: A trader should maintain discipline to wait for clearly defined trend conditions before entering or staying in a trade, rather than acting on incomplete setups or consensus opinion.
